# UC-19.2.31 · Nutanix Async Remote Site Replication Lag and RPO Risk

## Description

Protection domain replication lag directly affects recovery point objectives for DR sites. Sustained lag beyond policy means a regional failure could lose more data than the business expects, and backlogs slow catch-up after link restoration.

## Value

Protection domain replication lag directly affects recovery point objectives for DR sites. Sustained lag beyond policy means a regional failure could lose more data than the business expects, and backlogs slow catch-up after link restoration.

## Implementation

(1) Poll remote site and protection domain replication metrics from Prism; (2) set lag and snapshot-age thresholds from documented RPO; (3) alert when NearSync falls out of active or async lag exceeds SLA; (4) correlate with WAN utilization and snapshot schedules.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom (Nutanix Prism Element REST API).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=hci` `sourcetype="nutanix:remote_site"` with fields `cluster`, `remote_site`, `curator_repl_lag_sec`, `near_sync_status`, `last_successful_snapshot`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Poll remote site and protection domain replication metrics from Prism; (2) set lag and snapshot-age thresholds from documented RPO; (3) alert when NearSync falls out of active or async lag exceeds SLA; (4) correlate with WAN utilization and snapshot schedules.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="nutanix:remote_site" earliest=-4h
| eval snap_age_sec=now()-strptime(last_successful_snapshot,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| where curator_repl_lag_sec>600 OR snap_age_sec>3600 OR lower(near_sync_status)!="active"
| stats latest(curator_repl_lag_sec) as lag_sec, latest(snap_age_sec) as snap_age by cluster, remote_site
| sort -lag_sec
```

Understanding this SPL

**Nutanix Async Remote Site Replication Lag and RPO Risk** — Protection domain replication lag directly affects recovery point objectives for DR sites. Sustained lag beyond policy means a regional failure could lose more data than the business expects, and backlogs slow catch-up after link restoration.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=hci` `sourcetype="nutanix:remote_site"` with fields `cluster`, `remote_site`, `curator_repl_lag_sec`, `near_sync_status`, `last_successful_snapshot`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom (Nutanix Prism Element REST API). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hci; **sourcetype**: nutanix:remote_site. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hci, sourcetype="nutanix:remote_site", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **snap_age_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where curator_repl_lag_sec>600 OR snap_age_sec>3600 OR lower(near_sync_status)!="active"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cluster, remote_site** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Nutanix Prism (health, storage, and alerts) for the same cluster and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (sites over RPO), Line chart (replication lag), Single value (worst lag seconds).

## SPL

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="nutanix:remote_site" earliest=-4h
| eval snap_age_sec=now()-strptime(last_successful_snapshot,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| where curator_repl_lag_sec>600 OR snap_age_sec>3600 OR lower(near_sync_status)!="active"
| stats latest(curator_repl_lag_sec) as lag_sec, latest(snap_age_sec) as snap_age by cluster, remote_site
| sort -lag_sec
```

Table (sites over RPO), Line chart (replication lag), Single value (worst lag seconds).
